name: Ginger Carrots
subtitle: The gateway snack for kids and skeptics
blrNote: Bengaluru carrots are surprisingly sweet. Use the "red" winter carrots for a deeper flavor if they are in season.
tldr: Carrot sticks submerged in salt water with ginger. Ends up crunchier than fresh carrots.

steps.title: Slice
steps.instruction: Cut carrots into sticks that will fit vertically in your jar.

steps.title: Brine
steps.instruction: Mix 15g salt into 500ml water. Pour over carrots and ginger slices.

steps.title: Wait
steps.instruction: Ferment for 5–7 days. The brine will get cloudy—that’s the good stuff.

images.caption: Ginger Carrots
images.alt: Ginger Carrots — The gateway snack for kids and skeptics

culturalContext.story: A staple of the "Weston A. Price" style diet. It’s the easiest way to get probiotics into a sandwich.
culturalContext.significance: Carrots maintain their structural integrity better than almost any other vegetable when fermented.
culturalContext.funFact: The ginger prevents the carrots from smelling "swampy," keeping them fresh and bright.
